title: Butter author Asako Yuzuki: ‘I’m very far from the ideal Japanese woman’
description:
Her novel about a female serial killer was a global hit. As Asako Yuzuki’s second book is published in English, she talks about criticism at home – and why she’ll be writing darker stories in the future

The next time Japanese novelist Asako Yuzuki comes to the UK, she would like to bake some traditional Japanese muffins for Paul Hollywood on The Great British Bake Off, she says when we meet over video call. It is evening in Tokyo, where she lives with her partner and eight
hook: Japanese author on writing female killers, defying domestic expectations, and embracing darker narratives.

title: Christina Applegate’s New Memoir Is Furious, Funny and Profane
description: Funny, furious and profane, “You With the Sad Eyes” finds the TV star facing childhood trauma and reflecting on the limits imposed by illness.
hook: Christina Applegate's raw memoir confronts childhood trauma and illness's creative limits with humor and fury.

title: There are no psychopaths
description:
Virtually everything you think you know about psychopathy has been thoroughly debunked. Why does this zombie idea live on?

- by Rasmus Rosenberg Larsen
hook: Aeon dismantles the psychopath myth, revealing how psychology constructs dangerous categories of human behavior.

title: ‘Banquet for Fools’ Review: This Stunningly Tactile RPG Is a Bounty for the Senses
description:
The game spurs you to tap into, and relish, the nuance and dynamism of its combat.

The post ‘Banquet for Fools’ Review: This Stunningly Tactile RPG Is a Bounty for the Senses appeared first
hook: A tactile RPG game review that celebrates sensory richness and the nuance of interactive storytelling.

title: Going on Book Tour in Fiction and Then in Real Life
description:
When I was twenty-two, I came across Larissa Pham’s Pop Song: Adventures in Art and Intimacy in a bookstore in Oregon and could not put it down. Perhaps it was because the narrator was approximately the age I was and drew her experiences out in artistic fragments, phone calls, and reflections that gave shape to […]

The post Going on Book Tour in Fiction and Then in Real Life appeared
hook: Essay on Larissa Pham's fragmentary memoir and the experience of literary intimacy spanning fiction and reality.

title: On Angst
description: “What Lacan figures as structural alienation, Cixous writes as bodily anguish.”
hook: Paris Review explores angst through Lacan and Cixous, linking structural alienation to bodily experience and emotion.

title: Surprise on Ice
description: Obviously Canada considers the US its biggest rival. They’re playing in the gold medal game, and the Soviet Union no longer exists.
hook: n+1 on Olympic ice hockey rivalry and geopolitical meaning in sports narratives after Cold War collapse.
